title: Form Builder UI
description: React + Vite front-end for the Form Builder. Displays extracted fields and allows editing field labels, types, and required flags. Users can add and remove fields. All using GOV.UK Frontend components. No auth — hardcoded mock user.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite + React project scaffolded under /frontend with TypeScript
Main dashboard page: lists all forms fetched from GET /api/forms with formId, title, status, createdAt
Upload page: GOV.UK file-upload component posts PDF to POST /api/forms/upload
After upload, redirects to form editor page for the new form
Form editor page: displays field list from draft schema
Field edit: change label via text input, change type via select dropdown (text/textarea/date/number/radio/checkbox/select), toggle required via checkbox
Add field button: adds a new field row with default values at the end of the list
Delete field button: removes the field from the list with confirmation
Save button: PUT /api/forms/:formId/fields sends updated field array to backend
All form inputs use govuk-frontend components: text-input, select, checkboxes, buttons
All inputs have visible labels with correct htmlFor/id association
GOV.UK page structure: header with service name, phase banner (Beta), back link, main content, footer
Error display: inline errors under fields using govuk-frontend error pattern on save failure
Loading state: disable save button while saving
Full keyboard navigation through all controls
definitionOfDone: Form editor can load extracted fields, edit them, and save back to API
GOV.UK styling visible on all pages
Keyboard navigation works through the full edit workflow
Code passes TypeScript strict mode with no errors
Evaluator must run and produce a passing evaluation-02.json before sprint is marked complete
